title: Using the Agilent Genomic DNA 165 kb Kit on the Femto Pulse System
author: Jae Hee Jang
materials: If the volumes listed are inadequate:
For new stock tubes of 165 kb ladder:
For aliquoted tubes of 165 kb ladder:
Select OK to add method to the queue

steps.title: Dilute samples to appropriate concentrations using...
steps.instruction: Dilute samples to appropriate concentrations using distilled, nuclease-free water or 0.25x TE dilution buffer. Use the same diluent for all samples within a run Use 500 pg/µL for samples expected to have a wider distribution of various fragment sizes (e.g., HMW genomic DNA) Use 250 pg/µL for samples expected to have a more uniform size distribution (e.g., sheared DNA, DNA libraries)

steps.title: A 20 min Conditioning should be...
steps.instruction: A 20 min Conditioning should be done at the start of each day a run is put on the Femto Pulse system. To start the conditioning process:

steps.title: Before starting
steps.instruction: Before starting: Each row can hold up to 11 samples (well 12 should always contain the ladder). However, it is recommended to run more than one ladder per row so that if the ladder in well 12 does not look good, there is at least one back-up ladder to choose from. Loading a ladder from a separate file is not recommended

steps.title: For Drawer B of the Femto...
steps.instruction: For Drawer B of the Femto Pulse system, prepare a 96 deep well plate as follows:

steps.title: Check to see if the current...
steps.instruction: Check to see if the current solution levels shown on the Femto Pulse application are adequate. Solution levels shown on the Femto Pulse are not anutomatically detected and are based off initial input from the user. Confirm solution levels by visually inspecting the tubes .The following table lists the Femto Pulse system volume specifications Figure 1 : Depending on the number of samples, a certain volume of intercalating dye,separation gel, and 1x Conditioning solution are needed. For every 10 samples,add another 1.0 µL to the initial 1.0 µL intercalating dye and add another 10 mL to initial 10 mL Separation gel and 1x Conditioning Solution. There should be a maximum of 10 samples per row, as 2spots are needed per row for ladders

steps.title: For Drawer M of the Femto...
steps.instruction: For Drawer M of the Femto Pulse system, prepare a 96 well semi-skirted plate as follows:

steps.title: Place plates into their corresponding drawers
steps.instruction: Place plates into their corresponding drawers

steps.title: Using a clean 96-well sample plate,...
steps.instruction: Using a clean 96-well sample plate, pipette 18 µL of FP-8001 gDNA Diluent Marker Solution (DM) to each well of the 96-well plate to contain a sample

steps.title: Pipette 2 µL of each gDNA...
steps.instruction: Pipette 2 µL of each gDNA sample into the 18 µL of DM in the respective wells of the Sample Plate. Mix the sample wells by pipetting up and down three times with wide-bore genomic pipette tips

steps.title: Prepare the 165 kb Ladder Working Solution:
steps.instruction: Prepare the 165 kb Ladder Working Solution:

steps.title: Load 20 µL of the 165...
steps.instruction: Load 20 µL of the 165 kb Ladder Working Solution into Well 12 (and other wells, if duplicates of the ladder are being run) of the sample plate row that is to be analyzed

steps.title: For the tube of 1x Conditioning Solution
steps.instruction: For the tube of 1x Conditioning Solution: Combine 40 mL of distilled, nuclease-free water with 10 mL of 5x Conditioning Solution in a Falcon tube and invert to mix Using the freshly made 1x Conditioning Solution, fill the Conditioning Solution tube in the Femto Pulse system to the desired amount. Make sure to update the solution level on the Femto Pulse Excess 1x Conditioning Solution can be stored at Room temperature

steps.title: After loading the samples and 165...
steps.instruction: After loading the samples and 165 kb Ladder Working Ladder in each well, check the wells of the sample plate to ensure there are no air bubbles trapped in the bottom of the wells. Centrifuge the plate to remove any trapped air bubbles. The presence of trapped air bubbles can lead to injection failures

steps.title: For the tube of FP Large DNA Separation Gel
steps.instruction: For the tube of FP Large DNA Separation Gel: Combine the appropriate amounts of Separation Gel and Intercalating Dye according to the table shown in Step 2 in a Falcon tube Invert gently to mix Note: Each mixture of FP Large DNA Separation Gel is good for 2-3 weeks. Prepared gel that is currently not in use can be stored at 4 °C . Make sure to protect prepared gel from light

steps.title: Divide new, unused tubes of Diluent...
steps.instruction: Divide new, unused tubes of Diluent Marker into aliquots of around 1 mL each, in 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Use one tube at a time. Vortex to mix, then spin down Note: Diluent Marker is temperature-sensitive, and exposure to Room temperature should be limited to one hour at a time

steps.title: Row A
steps.instruction: Row A: 1.0 mL of 1x Inlet Buffer per well. This row should be replaced with fresh buffer daily, or every new day that the Femto Pulse system is run Note: the 165kb kit comes with 5x Inlet Buffer. Combine 40 mL of distilled water with 10 mL of the stock solution in a Falcon tube to make 1x Inlet Buffer

steps.title: Row H
steps.instruction: Row H: 1.0 mL of Capillary Storage Solution per well. This row of solution is good for one week, after which the whole plate should be replaced

steps.title: Row A
steps.instruction: Row A: 200 µL of 0.25x TE Rinse Buffer per well. This plate is to be replaced every new day that the Femto Pulse system is run

steps.title: Using the provided wide-bore pipette tips,...
steps.instruction: Using the provided wide-bore pipette tips, carefully mix the ladder up and down three times with the pipettor set to 20 µL

steps.title: Aliquot the 165 kb Ladder solution...
steps.instruction: Aliquot the 165 kb Ladder solution into 7 aliquots, 5 µL each, using the provided wide-bore genomic tips and the provided Eppendorf LoBind 0.5 mL tubes. Each aliquot can be used up to 4 times ( 1 µL per use)

steps.title: Store the 165 kb Ladder aliquots at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store the 165 kb Ladder aliquots at -20 °C

steps.title: Thaw the tube slowly on ice...
steps.instruction: Thaw the tube slowly on ice then briefly spin down to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube Note : The 165 kb ladder is extremely sensitive! Do not flick, vortex, or pipette mix using regular pipette tips. The ladder should be handled using only the wide-bore pipette tips provided in the 165 kb kit

steps.title: Thaw the tube slowly on ice
steps.instruction: Thaw the tube slowly on ice. Briefly spin down to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube. Do not flick, vortex, or pipette mix using regular pipette tips Note : Avoid freeze-thawing of the 165 kb Ladder more than 4 times (additional freeze-thawing may result in degradation of the higher molecular weight fragments in the 165 kb Ladder)

steps.title: In an Eppendorf LoBind 0
steps.instruction: In an Eppendorf LoBind 0.5 mL tube (provided), aliquot 9 µL of the DNF-498 0.25x TE Dilution Buffer

steps.title: To the same Eppendorf tube aliquot...
steps.instruction: To the same Eppendorf tube aliquot 90 µL of the FP-8001 gDNA Diluent Marker solution. Mix the contents of the tube by vortexing

steps.title: Mix the 165 kb Ladder aliquot...
steps.instruction: Mix the 165 kb Ladder aliquot very slowly by pipetting two times with a wide-bore genomic pipette tip and a pipettor set to 5 µL volume

steps.title: Using a regular pipette tip, immediately...
steps.instruction: Using a regular pipette tip, immediately aliquot 1 µL of the mixed 165 kb Ladder into the tube containing 99 µL of the solution from steps 13.1 and 13.2 above (DNF-498 0.25x TE Dilution Buffer + FP-8001 gDNA Diluent Marker). Do not pipette-mix or vortex

steps.title: Using the wide-bore genomic pipette tip...
steps.instruction: Using the wide-bore genomic pipette tip and a pipettor set to a 20 µL volume, slowly pipette the prepared 165 kb Ladder working solution up and down 5 times to mix Note : Use within one day of preparation

steps.title: To run the samples, place the...
steps.instruction: To run the samples, place the plate in one of the three sample plate trays (Drawers 4-6 from the top, labelled 1, 2, and 3) of the Femto Pulse instrument

steps.title: Run the sample plate immediately once...
steps.instruction: Run the sample plate immediately once prepared, or cover the sample plate with a cover film, store at 2 °C to 8 °C , and use as soon as possible

steps.title: Select Row, Group or Tray to run
steps.instruction: Select Row, Group or Tray to run. Input names of samples, as well as location of ladders and blanks

steps.title: Fill any unused wells within the...
steps.instruction: Fill any unused wells within the row of the sample plate with 20 µL of BF-P25 Blank Solution Note : The Blank Solution is light sensitive. Handle with care

steps.title: Select Add to Queue, from the...
steps.instruction: Select Add to Queue, from the dropdown menus select the corresponding method based on your capillary length: 3.1 FP-1002-22 – gDNA 165kb 3.2 FP-1002E-22 – Extended gDNA 165kb (preferred)

steps.title: Select the green Play icon to start the run
steps.instruction: Select the green Play icon to start the run

steps.title: Ensure that the ladder for each row is properly labelled
steps.instruction: Ensure that the ladder for each row is properly labelled. An extended gDNA 165kb ladder should look like the following: Figure 2 : the ideal extended gDNA 165 kb ladder

steps.title: From the main screen of the...
steps.instruction: From the main screen of the Femto Pulse control software, select the Operation tab. Under the Capillary Array > Conditioning field press Add to queue. The Select Conditioning Method form will be displayed, enabling the user to select the conditioning method from the dropdown menu

steps.title: Select the “20 min Conditioning” method from the dropdown menu
steps.instruction: Select the “20 min Conditioning” method from the dropdown menu. This method performs a 20 min conditioning solution flush followed by a 3 min Gel fill

steps.title: Press OK to add the method...
steps.instruction: Press OK to add the method to the instrument queue (press Cancel to abort adding the method)

steps.title: Press the green Play icon to...
steps.instruction: Press the green Play icon to start the sequence loaded into the queue

steps.title: Detected peaks can be added or...
steps.instruction: Detected peaks can be added or deleted by right-clicking in the location of the correct / incorrect peak. Choose option "Accept change" on the pop-up menu to confirm the new peak location. After accepting the ladder, size information for the samples will be generated
